relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Community conflict should not abandon sacred discernment.
Those called holy must learn to judge small matters with maturity.
If even messengers are judged, daily disputes need wisdom.
The assembly’s disputes reveal misplaced authority.
Paul asks whether no wise discerner exists among them.
Sibling dragging sibling before outsiders exposes communal defeat.
The lawsuits already show loss of love.
They wrong and defraud the very siblings they should protect.
Unjust patterns do not inherit the Divine reign.
Greed, theft, intoxicated harm, slander, and exploitation block inheritance.
The old patterns are not final: washed, sanctified, made just in Jesus and Spirit.
Freedom is not mastery by appetite.
The body is for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
Resurrection power includes us.
Bodies are Messiah’s members, not disposable instruments.
Sexual union carries body-level belonging.
Union with the Lord is one spirit.
Sexual misuse wounds the body’s own sacred belonging.
The body is temple of holy Spirit.
Bought with price, the body becomes glory-space for the Divine.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 6 is body-positive correction. The body is not dirty; it is raised, joined to Messiah, temple of Spirit, and worthy of non-exploitative love.
